translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The burden which Habakkuk the prophet did see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, how long shall I cry and thou wilt not hear? I cry out unto thee, Violence! and thou dost not save.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why dost thou cause me to see iniquity, and lookest thou upon grievance? For spoiling and violence are before me; and there is strife, and contention riseth up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore the law is powerless, and justice doth never go forth; for the wicked encompasseth the righteous; therefore judgment goeth forth perverted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See ye among the nations, and behold, and wonder marvellously; for [I] work a work in your days, which ye will not believe, though it be declared [to you].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, I raise up the Chaldeans, that bitter and impetuous nation, which marcheth through the breadth of the earth, to possess dwelling-places that are not theirs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are terrible and dreadful: their judgment and their dignity proceed from themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their horses are swifter than the leopards, and are more agile than the evening wolves; and their horsemen prance proudly, and their horsemen come from afar: they fly as an eagle that hasteth to devour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They come all of them for violence: the crowd of their faces is forwards, and they gather captives as the sand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, he scoffeth at kings, and princes are a scorn unto him; he derideth every stronghold: for he heapeth up dust, and taketh it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then will his mind change, and he will pass on, and become guilty: this his power is become hisgod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Art thou not from everlasting, Jehovah myGod, my Holy One? We shall not die. Jehovah, thou hast ordained him for judgment; and thou, O Rock, hast appointed him for correction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Thou art] of purer eyes than to behold evil, and canst not look on mischief: wherefore lookest thou upon them that deal treacherously, [and] keepest silence when the wicked swalloweth up a [man] more righteous than he?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou makest men as the fishes of the sea, as the creeping things, that have no ruler over them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He taketh up all of them with the hook, he catcheth them in his net, and gathereth them into his drag; therefore he rejoiceth and is glad:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore he sacrificeth unto his net, and burneth incense unto his drag; for by them his portion is become fat, and his meat dainty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall he therefore empty his net, and not spare to slay the nations continually?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will stand upon my watch, and set me upon the tower, and will look forth to see what he will say unto me, and what I shall answer as to my reproof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah answered me and said, Write the vision, and engrave it upon tablets, that he may run that readeth it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the vision is yet for an appointed time, but it hasteth to the end, and shall not lie: though it tarry, wait for it; for it will surely come, it will not delay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, his soul is puffed up, it is not upright within him: but the just shall live by his faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And moreover, the wine is treacherous: he is a proud man, and keepeth not at rest, he enlargeth his desire as Sheol, and he is like death and cannot be satisfied; and he assembleth unto him all nations, and gathereth unto him all peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall not all these take up a proverb about him, and a taunting riddle against him, and say, Woe to him that increaseth that which is not his! how long? — and to him that loadeth himself with pledges!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall they not rise up suddenly that shall bite thee, and they awake up that shall vex thee, and thou shalt be for booties unto them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because thou hast plundered many nations, all the rest of the peoples shall plunder thee; because of men's blood, and for the violence [done] to the land, to the city, and all that dwell therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to him that getteth iniquitous gain to his house, that he may set his nest on high, that he may be delivered from the grasp of evil!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast devised shame to thy house, by cutting off many peoples, and hast sinned against thine own soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the stone shall cry out of the wall, and the beam out of the timber shall answer it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to him that buildeth a town with blood, and establisheth a city by unrighteousness!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, is it not of Jehovah of hosts that the peoples labour for the fire, and the nations weary themselves in vain?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of Jehovah as the waters cover the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe unto him that giveth his neighbour drink, — that pourest out thy flask, and makest [him] drunken also, that thou mayest look on their nakedness!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou art filled with shame instead of glory; drink thou also, and let thy foreskin be uncovered: the cup of Jehovah's right hand shall be turned unto thee, and a shameful spewing shall be on thy glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the violence [done] to Lebanon shall cover thee, and the destruction of beasts which made them afraid; because of men's blood, and for the violence [done] to the land, to the city, and all that dwell therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What profiteth the graven image that the maker thereof hath graven it? the molten image, and the teacher of falsehood, that the maker of his work dependeth thereon, to make dumb idols?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe unto him that saith to the wood, Awake! to the dumb stone, Arise! Shall it teach? Behold it is overlaid with gold and silver, and there is no breath at all in the midst of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah is in his holy temple. Let all the earth keep silence before him!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A Prayer of Habakkuk the prophet upon Shigionoth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, I heard the report of thee, [and] I feared. Jehovah, revive thy work in the midst of the years, In the midst of the years make [it] known: In wrath remember mercy!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God came from Teman, And the Holy One from mount Paran. Selah. His glory covereth the heavens, And the earth is full of his praise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [his] brightness was as the light; Rays [came forth] from his hand; And there was the hiding of his power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before him went the pestilence, And a burning flame went forth at his feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He stood, and measured the earth; He beheld, and discomfited the nations; And the eternal mountains were scattered, The everlasting hills gave way: His ways are everlasting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction; The curtains of the land of Midian did tremble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Was Jehovah wrathful with the rivers? Was thine anger against the rivers? Was thy rage against the sea, That thou didst ride upon thy horses, Thy chariots of salvation?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy bow was made naked, The rods [of discipline] sworn according to [thy] word. Selah. Thou didst cleave the earth with rivers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The mountains saw thee, they were in travail: Torrents of waters passed by; The deep uttered its voice, Lifted up its hands on high.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sun [and] moon stood still in their habitation, At the light of thine arrows which shot forth, — At the shining of thy glittering spear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst march through the land in indignation, Thou didst thresh the nations in anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou wentest forth for the salvation of thy people, For the salvation of thine anointed; Thou didst smite off the head from the house of the wicked, Laying bare the foundation even to the neck. Selah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst strike through with his own spears the head of his leaders: They came out as a whirlwind to scatter me, Whose exulting was as to devour the afflicted secretly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou didst walk through the sea with thy horses, The heap of great waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I heard, and my belly trembled; My lips quivered at the voice; Rottenness entered into my bones, and I trembled in my place, That I might rest in the day of distress, When their invader shall come up against the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For though the fig-tree shall not blossom, Neither shall fruit be in the vines; The labour of the olive-tree shall fail, And the fields shall yield no food; The flock shall be cut off from the fold, And there shall be no herd in the stalls:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet I will rejoice in Jehovah, I will joy in theGod of my salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, the Lord, is my strength, And he maketh my feet like hinds' [feet], And he will make me to walk upon my high places. To the chief Musician. On my stringed instruments.
